title: The Lenape make Lenapehoking their homeland
book: The Dutch-Munsee Encounter in America: The Struggle for Sovereignty in the Hudson Valley
author: Paul Otto
note: A prize-winning scholarly study of the Munsee-speaking Lenape and their world, tracing Indigenous life and sovereignty from first contact through Dutch settlement.

title: Henry Hudson explores the river that bears his name
book: The Island at the Center of the World: The Epic Story of Dutch Manhattan and the Forgotten Colony That Shaped America
author: Russell Shorto
note: The definitive popular narrative of Dutch New York, opening with Hudson's voyage and drawing on the newly translated colonial records to argue New Netherland shaped America.

title: Dutch West India Company is chartered
book: The Colony of New Netherland: A Dutch Settlement in Seventeenth-Century America
author: Jaap Jacobs
note: The leading scholarly history of the colony and the Dutch West India Company that governed it, from the first trading voyages to the English takeover.

title: The Schaghen letter records the 'purchase' of Manhattan
book: Gotham: A History of New York City to 1898
author: Edwin G. Burrows and Mike Wallace
note: The Pulitzer Prize-winning single-volume history of the city, authoritative on the 1626 'purchase' and the gulf between Dutch and Lenape ideas of ownership.

title: First enslaved Africans brought to New Amsterdam
book: In the Shadow of Slavery: African Americans in New York City, 1626-1863
author: Leslie M. Harris
note: The most comprehensive history of Black New York, beginning with the first enslaved Africans the Dutch West India Company brought to New Amsterdam in the 1620s.

title: Kieft's War erupts with the Pavonia massacre
book: The Dutch-Munsee Encounter in America: The Struggle for Sovereignty in the Hudson Valley
author: Paul Otto
note: This Hendricks Award-winning study explains the deteriorating Dutch-Munsee relations that culminated in Kieft's War and the Pavonia massacre.

title: Peter Stuyvesant arrives as director-general
book: The Island at the Center of the World: The Epic Story of Dutch Manhattan and the Forgotten Colony That Shaped America
author: Russell Shorto
note: Shorto's acclaimed narrative centers on Stuyvesant's autocratic rule and his rivalry with reformer Adriaen van der Donck over the colony's future.

title: The English seize New Amsterdam, renaming it New York
book: The Colony of New Netherland: A Dutch Settlement in Seventeenth-Century America
author: Jaap Jacobs
note: Jacobs's authoritative account carries the colony's political and economic story through to the 1664 surrender that made New Amsterdam into New York.

title: The 1712 slave revolt
book: Black and White Manhattan: The History of Racial Formation in Colonial New York City
author: Thelma Wills Foote
note: A rigorous scholarly account of how racial hierarchy was built in colonial New York, covering the 1712 revolt and the harsh slave codes that followed.

title: The Zenger trial and freedom of the press
book: A Brief Narrative of the Case and Tryal of John Peter Zenger: with Related Documents
author: Paul Finkelman (editor)
note: The standard teaching edition of the trial's own record, pairing the primary 1736 narrative with a legal historian's introduction on its place in press freedom.

title: The 1741 conspiracy trials
book: New York Burning: Liberty, Slavery, and Conspiracy in Eighteenth-Century Manhattan
author: Jill Lepore
note: A Pulitzer finalist and the definitive modern study of the 1741 'conspiracy,' reconstructing the panic, the trials and the executions that followed.

title: King's College, later Columbia, is founded
author: Ron Chernow
note: Chernow's landmark biography follows a young King's College student who became a founder, giving vivid texture to Revolutionary-era New York.

title: The Sons of Liberty organize in New York
book: The Battle for New York: The City at the Heart of the American Revolution
author: Barnet Schecter
note: A well-reviewed narrative history that opens with the 1760s radicalism of Isaac Sears and the Sons of Liberty and follows the city through the war.

title: The Battle of Long Island
book: The Battle for New York: The City at the Heart of the American Revolution
author: Barnet Schecter
note: The dramatic heart of Schecter's book is the 1776 campaign, whose largest engagement, the Battle of Long Island, cost Washington the city.

title: The prison ships of Wallabout Bay
book: Forgotten Patriots: The Untold Story of American Prisoners During the Revolutionary War
author: Edwin G. Burrows
note: The first full account of the British prison ships in Wallabout Bay, where more Americans died than in all the war's battles combined.

title: Evacuation Day ends British occupation
book: Gotham: A History of New York City to 1898
author: Edwin G. Burrows and Mike Wallace
note: The Pulitzer Prize-winning history sets Evacuation Day and Washington's triumphant return within the full sweep of the city's founding centuries.
